eval.summary: A Claude Code engineer shares three skill-building tips: (1) Add a 'gotchas' section to each skill file — a living list of mistakes Claude has made that you don't want repeated, updated over time as errors occur. (2) Structure skills as folders, not single markdown files — include scripts, references, examples, and templates alongside the prompt; Claude pulls these in only as needed, avoiding context window bloat. (3) Avoid over-constraining Claude — give context and constraints but leave room for adaptation at invocation time. The video is a distillation of practical patterns for improving Claude's skill execution quality over time.

patterns.description: Add a 'Gotchas' section to every skill file (and CLAUDE.md) — a continuously updated list of past mistakes Claude made that should not recur
patterns.apply_to: CLAUDE.md top-level and every .lux/skills/*.md or equivalent skill file — append gotchas after each session where an error or unwanted behavior occurred

patterns.description: Restructure skills as folders: one folder per skill containing the prompt .md, plus subfiles for scripts, references, examples, and templates — Claude pulls subfiles in only when needed
patterns.apply_to: .lux/skills/ directory structure — replace flat .md files with named folders; orchestrator.py skill loader should reference folder root and let Claude lazily load subfiles

patterns.description: Write skill instructions with context and constraints but not rigid step sequences — leave invocation-time adaptation room so Claude isn't railroaded into brittle paths
patterns.apply_to: All skill prompt authoring going forward; audit existing CLAUDE.md instructions for over-specification and soften imperative step lists into outcome-oriented guidance

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: pattern only — no tool; complements existing CLAUDE.md + .lux/Data memory architecture
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Lens 1 drives this verdict entirely. All three patterns are immediately applicable to our existing stack with zero new tooling required. The gotchas pattern in particular is a direct behavioral improvement loop for any skill file we ship. Folder-as-skill structure directly extends our .lux/ architecture.
action_items: Add a '## Gotchas' section to CLAUDE.md today — seed with known past errors
Create .lux/skills/ folder structure: mkdir per skill, move existing .md prompts inside, add examples/ and templates/ subdirs
Audit top 3 most-used skill prompts for over-rigid step sequences and rewrite as outcome-oriented with constraints

strategic_signal.signal: The 'folder-as-skill with progressive disclosure' pattern is emerging as a community-level standard for Claude Code skill authoring — Veritas should codify this as the canonical skill structure before it becomes an external framework we'd have to adopt on someone else's terms.
